requirements.full_sentence:
Chapter I: Preliminary
Explanation: In this context, urban co-operative banks shall mean Primary Co-
operative Banks as defined under Section 5(ccv) read with Section 56 of
Banking Regulation Act, 1949.
requirements.object: Banks as defined under Section 5(ccv) read with Section 56 of Banking Regulation Act, 1949

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative
Banks - Classification, Valuation and Operation of Investment Portfolio)
Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Classification, Valuation and Operation of Investment Portfolio) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence: These Directions shall come into effect from the date of issue.
requirements.object: the date of issue

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks
(hereinafter collectively referred to as 'UCBs' and individually as a 'UCB').
requirements.object: (Urban) Co-operative Banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'UCBs' and individually as a 'UCB')

All other expressions unless defined herein shall have the same meaning as

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board of Directors (Board) shall approve the Investment Policy of a UCB
as specified in paragraph 20.
requirements.object: of a UCB as specified in paragraph 20

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall approve the shifting of Investments among various categories
(viz., HTM, AFS, and HFT) as specified in paragraphs 42 to 49.
requirements.object: Investments among various categories (viz

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall also ensure that Investment Policy of a UCB provides for
operations being conducted in accordance with the norms laid down by
Reserve Bank / SEBI and the respective exchange as specified in paragraph
92.
requirements.object: Policy of a UCB provides for operations being conducted in accordance with the norms laid down by Reserve Bank / SEBI and

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall review the various aspects of non-SLR investment at least on
quarterly basis as specified in paragraph 119.
requirements.object: of non-SLR investment at least on quarterly basis as specified in paragraph 119

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall approve the policy for placement of deposits with other banks
and shall review the position at least on a half yearly basis as specified in
paragraph 132.
requirements.object: placement of deposits with other banks and shall review the position at least on a half yearly basis as specified in paragraph

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall approve the internal control guidelines for acquisition of non-
SLR securities in the secondary market as specified in paragraph 133(12).
requirements.object: guidelines for acquisition of non- SLR securities in the secondary market as specified in paragraph 133(12)

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall approve and annually review the panel of brokers with whom a
UCB may engage in business as specified in paragraph 135.
requirements.object: the panel of brokers with whom a UCB may engage in business as specified in paragraph 135

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall be informed post facto about breach of aggregate upper
contract limit for individual broker as specified in paragraph 139(3).
requirements.object: about breach of aggregate upper contract limit for individual broker as specified in paragraph 139(3)

requirements.full_sentence:
The monthly reconciliation of the SGL / CSGL account balance with the Public
Debt Office (PDO) shall be periodically checked by the internal auditors and
placed before the Audit Committee of the Board (ACB) as specified in
paragraph 143.
requirements.object: the internal auditors and placed before the Audit Committee of the Board (ACB) as specified in paragraph 143

requirements.full_sentence:
The ACB shall review the details of trades on aggregate basis done on the
exchanges and any transaction that were 'closed-out' on the exchanges as
specified in paragraph 144.
requirements.object: trades on aggregate basis done on the exchanges and any transaction that were 'closed-out' on the exchanges as specified in para

requirements.full_sentence:
The results of internal audit pertaining to investment functions shall be placed
before the Board once in every quarter as specified in paragraph 145.
requirements.object: Board once in every quarter as specified in paragraph 145

requirements.full_sentence:
A copy of the monthly review of investment transactions, including the details of
large transactions shall be put up to the Board once a month, for information as
specified in paragraph 146.
requirements.object: the Board once a month, for information as specified in paragraph 146

requirements.full_sentence:
A half-yearly review (as of March 31st and September 30th) of the investment
portfolio shall be placed before the Board within two months, i.e., by end-May
and end-November as specified in paragraph 148.
requirements.object: Board within two months, i

requirements.full_sentence:
With the approval of the Board, a UCB may, at its discretion, build up a higher
percentage of Investment Fluctuation Reserve (IFR) depending on the size and
composition of its portfolio as specified in paragraph 153(4).
requirements.object: build up a higher percentage of Investment Fluctuation Reserve (IFR) depending on the size and composition of its portfolio as sp

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall adopt a comprehensive Investment Policy duly approved by the
Board.
requirements.object: Policy duly approved by the Board

requirements.full_sentence:
The Investment Policy shall be in accordance with the size, complexity
of business, risk management capabilities, human resource, and IT
infrastructure, and other such factors relevant for a UCB.
requirements.object: the size, complexity of business, risk management capabilities, human resource, and IT infrastructure, and other such factors re

requirements.full_sentence:
The Investment Policy shall, at the minimum, include:
(1) The investment criteria and objectives to be achieved while undertaking
investment transactions, keeping in view the various regulatory / statutory
guidelines and a UCB's own internal requirements;
requirements.object: include: (1) The investment criteria and objectives to be achieved while undertaking investment transactions, keeping in view the va

requirements.full_sentence: (2) Securities in which investments may be made;

and
(9) Proper risk management systems for making investment in non-SLR
securities which shall include entry-level minimum credit ratings / quality
standards and industry-wise, maturity-wise, duration-wise, issuer-wise,
<image>
etc., limits to mitigate the adverse impact of concentration, and liquidity
risk.
requirements.object: ratings / quality standards and industry-wise, maturity-wise, duration-wise, issuer-wise, <image> etc

requirements.full_sentence: The Investment Policy shall be reviewed at least annually.
requirements.object: annually

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall undertake all transactions in securities only on its own investment
account.
requirements.object: securities only on its own investment account

requirements.full_sentence:
It shall not undertake any transactions on behalf of Portfolio
Management Scheme (PMS) clients in its fiduciary capacity, and on behalf of
other clients, either as custodian of their investments or purely as their agent.
requirements.object: behalf of Portfolio Management Scheme (PMS) clients in its fiduciary capacity, and on behalf of other clients, either as

requirements.full_sentence:
Investment proposals shall be subjected to the same degree of credit risk
analysis as any loan proposal.
requirements.object: same degree of credit risk analysis as any loan proposal

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall refer to the list of defaulters obtained from Credit Information
Companies while taking investment decisions.
requirements.object: of defaulters obtained from Credit Information Companies while taking investment decisions

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall make its own internal credit analysis and credit rating even in
respect of rated issues and shall not entirely rely on the ratings of external
credit rating agencies.
requirements.object: ratings of external credit rating agencies

requirements.full_sentence:
The appraisal shall be more stringent in respect of
investments in instruments issued by non-borrower customers.
requirements.object: respect of investments in instruments issued by non-borrower customers

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall ensure robust internal credit rating systems which shall also
include building up of a system of regular (quarterly or half-yearly) tracking of
the financial position of the issuer to ensure continuous monitoring of the rating
migration of the issuers / issues.
requirements.object: rating systems which shall also include building up of a system of regular (quarterly or half-yearly) tracking of the fi

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall settle the transactions in securities and derivatives, wherever
allowed, as per procedure prescribed in notifications /directions / guidelines
issued by the concerned regulator.
requirements.object: securities and derivatives

requirements.full_sentence: A UCB shall hold its investments in securities only in dematerialized form.
requirements.object: securities only in dematerialized form

requirements.full_sentence:
Unless permitted specifically in these Directions, a UCB shall not hold a short
position in any security.
requirements.object: in any security

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall classify its entire investment portfolio including SLR securities and
non-SLR securities under three categories, viz., 'Held to Maturity' (HTM),
'Available for Sale' (AFS), and 'Held for Trading' (HFT).
requirements.object: portfolio including SLR securities and non-SLR securities under three categories, viz

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall decide the category of the investment at the time of acquisition
and the decision shall be recorded on the investment proposals.
requirements.object: the investment at the time of acquisition and the decision shall be recorded on the investment proposals

requirements.full_sentence:
Securities acquired by a UCB with the intention to hold them up to maturity
shall be classified under HTM category.
requirements.object: category

requirements.full_sentence:
Investments under HTM category shall not exceed 25 per cent of a UCB's total
investments.
requirements.object: of a UCB's total investments

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that such bonds shall have minimum residual maturity of seven
years at the time of investment.
requirements.object: of seven years at the time of investment

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided further that a UCB shall have the option to continue to classify
these investments under HTM category even if the residual maturity falls
below seven years subsequently.
requirements.object: continue to classify these investments under HTM category even if the residual maturity falls below seven years subsequently

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall have the option to exceed the limit of 25 per cent of its total
investments under HTM category provided the excess comprises of SLR
securities.
requirements.object: exceed the limit of 25 per cent of its total investments under HTM category provided the excess comprises of SLR securities

requirements.full_sentence:
However, the total SLR securities held in the HTM category shall not
be more than 25 per cent of a UCB's Net Demand and Time Liabilities (NDTL)
as on the last Friday of the second preceding fortnight.
requirements.object: per cent of a UCB's Net Demand and Time Liabilities (NDTL) as on the last Friday of the second preceding fortnight

requirements.full_sentence:
Profit on sale of investments from HTM category shall be first taken to the Profit
and Loss Account, and thereafter shall be appropriated to the 'Capital Reserve'.
requirements.object: the Profit and Loss Account, and thereafter shall be appropriated to the 'Capital Reserve'

requirements.full_sentence:
The amount so appropriated shall be net of taxes and the amount required to
be transferred to Statutory Reserves.
requirements.object: and the amount required to be transferred to Statutory Reserves

requirements.full_sentence:
Loss on sale shall be recognized in the
Profit and Loss Account in the year of sale.
requirements.object: Profit and Loss Account in the year of sale

requirements.full_sentence:
Securities acquired with the intention to trade by taking advantage of the short-
term price / interest rate movements shall be classified under HFT category.
requirements.object: category

requirements.full_sentence: The investments classified under HFT shall be sold within 90 days.
requirements.object: days

requirements.full_sentence:
Securities which do not fall under HTM or HFT categories shall be classified
under AFS category.
requirements.object: category

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall have the option to decide on the extent of investment holdings
under AFS and HFT category taking into account various aspects.
requirements.object: decide on the extent of investment holdings under AFS and HFT category taking into account various aspects

requirements.full_sentence:
Profit or loss on sale of investments in HFT and AFS categories shall be taken
to the Profit and Loss Account.
requirements.object: Profit and Loss Account

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall have the option to shift investments to / from HTM category with
the approval of the Board once a year.
requirements.object: shift investments to / from HTM category with the approval of the Board once a year

conditions:
Provided that such shifting shall be done at the beginning of the accounting
year
requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that such shifting shall be done at the beginning of the accounting
year.
requirements.object: beginning of the accounting year

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided further that additional shifting to / from HTM category shall not be
done during the remaining part of that accounting year.
requirements.object: remaining part of that accounting year

requirements.full_sentence:
Transfer of securities from AFS / HFT category to HTM category shall be made
at the lower of book value or market value.
requirements.object: lower of book value or market value

conditions:
Provided that where the market value is higher than the book value at the time
of transfer
requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that where the market value is higher than the book value at the time
of transfer, the appreciation shall be ignored, and the security shall be
transferred at the book value.
requirements.object: security shall be transferred at the book value

conditions:
where the market value is lower than the book
value
requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
Provided further that in cases where the market value is lower than the book
value, the provision for depreciation held against the security (including the
additional provision, if any, required based on valuation done on the date of
transfer) shall be adjusted to reduce the book value to the market value and the
security shall be transferred at the market value.
requirements.object: the book value to the market value and the security shall be transferred at the market value

requirements.full_sentence:
Transfer of securities from HTM to AFS / HFT category shall be subject to the
following conditions:
(1) Security originally placed under the HTM category at a discount, shall be
transferred to AFS / HFT category at the acquisition price / book value.
requirements.object: following conditions: (1) Security originally placed under the HTM category at a discount, shall be transferred to AFS / HFT categor

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Security originally placed under the HTM category at a premium shall be
transferred to the AFS / HFT category at the amortised cost.
requirements.object: AFS / HFT category at the amortised cost

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Securities shall be immediately re-valued consequent to transfer and
resultant depreciation, if any, shall be provided.
requirements.object: to transfer and resultant depreciation

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: Regarding 44(1) above, a UCB shall not accrue the discount
on the securities held under HTM category and such securities shall be
held at acquisition cost till maturity.
requirements.object: the securities held under HTM category and such securities shall be held at acquisition cost till maturity

requirements.full_sentence: A UCB shall not sell securities held in HTM category.
requirements.object: HTM category

conditions:
if due to
liquidity stress
requirements.full_sentence:
However, if due to
liquidity stress, a UCB is required to sell securities from HTM portfolio, it shall
do so with the permission of the Board and rationale for such sale shall be
clearly recorded.
requirements.object: permission of the Board and rationale for such sale shall be clearly recorded

conditions: In case of transfers of securities to / from HTM category
requirements.full_sentence:
In case of transfers of securities to / from HTM category, a UCB shall make
disclosure in the 'Notes to Accounts' to the Financial Statements as provided in
Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Financial Statements:
Presentation and Disclosures) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: 'Notes to Accounts' to the Financial Statements as provided in Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Financial Stat

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall have the option to shift investments from AFS category to HFT
category with the approval of the Board.
requirements.object: shift investments from AFS category to HFT category with the approval of the Board

conditions: Provided that in case of exigencies
requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that in case of exigencies, shifting can be done with the approval of
the Chief Executive of a UCB, but shall be ratified by the Board.
requirements.object: Board

requirements.full_sentence:
Shifting of investments from HFT category to AFS category shall not be
permitted.

conditions:
where a UCB is not in a position to sell the security within 90 days due to tight
liquidity conditions
requirements.timeline:
within 90 days due to tight
liquidity conditions
requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the above prohibition shall not apply in exceptional circumstances
where a UCB is not in a position to sell the security within 90 days due to tight
liquidity conditions, or extreme volatility, or market becoming unidirectional.
requirements.object: where a UCB is not in a position to sell the security within 90 days due to tight liquidity conditions, or extreme

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided further that such transfer shall be done only with the approval of the
Board / Investment Committee.
requirements.object: the approval of the Board / Investment Committee

requirements.full_sentence:
In the case of transfer of securities from AFS to HFT category or vice-versa, the
securities need not be re-valued on the date of transfer and the provisions for
the accumulated depreciation, if any, held shall be transferred to the provisions
for depreciation against the HFT securities and vice-versa.
requirements.object: provisions for depreciation against the HFT securities and vice-versa

conditions:
provided that it is less than
the face value of the security
requirements.full_sentence:
The investment shall be carried at acquisition cost provided that it is less than
the face value of the security.
requirements.object: cost provided that it is less than the face value of the security

conditions: If acquisition cost is more than face value
requirements.full_sentence:
If acquisition cost is more than face value, the premium arising out of difference
between face value and acquisition cost shall be amortised over the period
remaining to maturity.
requirements.object: period remaining to maturity

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: The book value of the security shall continue to be reduced to the
extent of the amount amortised during the relevant accounting period.
requirements.object: to the extent of the amount amortised during the relevant accounting period

requirements.full_sentence:
The individual securities in the HFT category shall be marked to market at
monthly or at more frequent intervals.
requirements.object: at monthly or at more frequent intervals

requirements.full_sentence:
The individual securities in the AFS category shall be marked to market at
quarterly or at more frequent intervals.
requirements.object: at quarterly or at more frequent intervals

requirements.full_sentence:
The book value of individual securities in HFT and AFS categories shall not
undergo any change after marking to market.
requirements.object: marking to market

requirements.full_sentence:
Securities under AFS and HFT categories shall be valued security-wise and
depreciation / appreciation shall be aggregated for purpose of arriving at net
depreciation / appreciation of investments for each classification, viz., a)
Government Securities, b) other approved securities, c) shares, d) corporate
bonds, and e) others (to be specified)), separately for AFS and HFT categories.
requirements.object: depreciation / appreciation shall be aggregated for purpose of arriving at net depreciation / appreciation of investments

requirements.full_sentence: Net depreciation, if any, shall be provided for.

Net appreciation, if any, shall be
ignored.

Net depreciation required to be provided for in any one classification shall not
be reduced on account of net appreciation in any other classification.
requirements.object: of net appreciation in any other classification

requirements.full_sentence:
Chapter VI: Market Value
The 'market value' for the purpose of periodical valuation of investments
included in the AFS and the HFT categories shall be as under:

The 'market value' for the quoted securities shall be the prices declared by the
Financial Benchmarks India Pvt.
requirements.object: by the Financial Benchmarks India Pvt

requirements.full_sentence:
For securities whose prices are not published by FBIL, market price of
the quoted security shall be as available from the trades / quotes on the
exchanges / reporting platforms / trading platforms authorized by RBI / SEBI
and prices declared by Fixed Income Money Market and Derivatives
Association of India (FIMMDA).

requirements.full_sentence:
Central Government Securities

the prices / YTM rates put out by the FBIL.

State Government Securities
State Government Securities shall be valued on the basis of the prices / YTM
rates put out by FBIL.
requirements.object: basis of the prices / YTM rates put out by FBIL

requirements.full_sentence:
Other Approved Securities
Other approved securities shall be valued applying the YTM method by marking
it up by 25 basis points above the yields of the Central Government Securities
of equivalent maturity put out by FBIL.
requirements.object: YTM method by marking it up by 25 basis points above the yields of the Central Government Securities of equivalent maturity put

requirements.full_sentence:
Corporate Bonds
(1) All debentures / bonds shall be valued on the YTM basis.
requirements.object: YTM basis

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(2) Debentures / bonds shall be valued by applying the appropriate mark-up
over the YTM rates for Central Government Securities as put out by FBIL
/ FIMMDA.
requirements.object: the appropriate mark-up over the YTM rates for Central Government Securities as put out by FBIL / FIMMDA

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The mark-up applied shall be determined based on the ratings assigned
to the debentures / bonds by the credit rating agencies and shall be
subject to the following:
i The mark-up shall be at least 50 basis points above the rate applicable
to a Central Government Security of equivalent maturity for rated
debenture / bonds.
requirements.object: the ratings assigned to the debentures / bonds by the credit rating agencies and shall be subject to the following: i The mark-

requirements.full_sentence:
ii The rate used for the YTM for un-rated debentures / bonds shall not be
less than the rate applicable to rated debentures / bonds of equivalent
maturity.
requirements.object: rate applicable to rated debentures / bonds of equivalent maturity

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the mark-up for the un-rated debentures / bonds should
appropriately reflect the credit risk borne by the UCB.
requirements.object: un-rated debentures / bonds should appropriately reflect the credit risk borne by the UCB

conditions:
Where the debentures / bonds are quoted and there have been
transactions within 15 days prior to the valuation date
requirements.timeline: within 15 days prior to the valuation date
requirements.full_sentence:
iii Where the debentures / bonds are quoted and there have been
transactions within 15 days prior to the valuation date, the value
adopted shall not be higher than the rate at which the transaction is
recorded on the exchanges / trading platforms / reporting platforms
authorized by SEBI / RBI.
requirements.object: rate at which the transaction is recorded on the exchanges / trading platforms / reporting platforms authorized by SEBI / RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
Special Securities issued by the Government of India
The special securities [i.e., Oil Bonds, Fertiliser Bonds, bonds issued to the
State Bank of India (during the 2008 rights issue), Industrial Finance
Corporation of India Ltd., and Food Corporation of India], which are directly
issued by the Government of India, and which do not carry SLR status, shall be
valued at a spread of 25 basis points above the corresponding yield on Central
Government Securities.
requirements.object: spread of 25 basis points above the corresponding yield on Central Government Securities

requirements.full_sentence:
Units of Mutual Funds
(1) Investments in un-quoted mutual funds units shall be valued on the basis
of the latest re-purchase price declared by the Asset Management
Company in respect of each Scheme.
requirements.object: basis of the latest re-purchase price declared by the Asset Management Company in respect of each Scheme

conditions: In case of funds with a lock-in period or any other fund

where repurchase
price / market quote is not available
requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(2) In case of funds with a lock-in period or any other fund, where repurchase
price / market quote is not available, units shall be valued at Net Asset
Value (NAV) of the scheme.
requirements.object: Asset Value (NAV) of the scheme

conditions: If NAV is not available
requirements.full_sentence:
If NAV is not available, then these shall be
valued at cost, till the end of the lock-in period.
requirements.object: till the end of the lock-in period

requirements.full_sentence:
Commercial Paper and Certificate of Deposits
Commercial paper and Certificate of Deposits shall be valued at the carrying
cost.
requirements.object: carrying cost

requirements.full_sentence:
Zero coupon bonds (ZCBs)
(1) ZCBs shall be valued in the books at carrying cost which shall be
computed by adding the acquisition cost and discount accrued at the rate
prevailing at the time of acquisition, which shall be marked to market with
reference to the market value.
requirements.object: books at carrying cost which shall be computed by adding the acquisition cost and discount accrued at the rate prevailing at the time

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) In the absence of market value, the ZCBs shall be marked to market with
reference to the present value of the ZCB.
requirements.object: with reference to the present value of the ZCB

Explanation: The present value of the ZCBs may be calculated by
discounting the face value using the 'Zero Coupon Yield Curve', with
appropriate mark up as per the zero-coupon spreads put out by FIMMDA
/ FBIL.
requirements.object: the face value using the 'Zero Coupon Yield Curve', with appropriate mark up as per the zero-coupon spreads put out by FI

In case the UCB is still carrying the ZCB at acquisition cost, the

value of the bond, before marking it to market.
requirements.object: the book value of the bond, before marking it to market

Security Receipts (SRs) / Pass Through Certificates (PTCs) / other securities

Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Transfer and Distribution
of Credit Risk) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Transfer and Distribution of Credit Risk) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
Shares of Co-operative Institutions
(1) Investment in shares of co-operative institutions from which a UCB has
regularly received dividends shall be valued at face value.
requirements.object: value

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(2) A UCB shall make full provision in respect of its investments in shares of
co-operative institutions which have either gone into liquidation or have
not declared dividend at all.
requirements.object: respect of its investments in shares of co-operative institutions which have either gone into liquidation or have not declared

conditions:
In case the latest Balance Sheet of co-operative institutions is not
available for more than 18 months

(3) In case the latest Balance Sheet of co-operative institutions is not
available for more than 18 months, the shares shall be valued at ₹1/- per
co-operative institution.
requirements.object: per co-operative institution

when the

For the purpose of maintaining Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR) required under

UCB shall refer to the Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks -
Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) Directions, 2025.

While undertaking OTC transactions in government securities, a UCB shall
seek a scheduled commercial bank, a Primary Dealer (PD), a financial

with such counter parties.

Guidelines for Primary Dealers.

if completed through the securities / funds shortage handling procedure of

time.

requirements.object: Issued Transactions (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2018

as follows:

item till issue of the security;

security.

In case the price of the 'WI' security is not available, the value of

if any, shall

and

Value Free Transfer (VFT) in government securities shall be in terms of 'Value
Free Transfer (VFT) of Government Securities - Guidelines' issued vide
IDMD.CDD.No.S930/11.22.003/2021-22 dated October 5, 2021, as amended

A UCB shall have the option to undertake transactions in government securities
through exchanges, in addition to the existing mode of dealing through SGL /
CSGL accounts with Reserve Bank or gilt accounts with the designated entities.

For this purpose, a UCB shall have the option to open a demat account with a
bank depository participant (DP) of NSDL / CDSL or with SHCIL.

The Board of a UCB shall also ensure that Investment Policy of the UCB
provides for operations being conducted in accordance with the norms laid
down by Reserve Bank / SEBI and the respective exchange.

trading and settlement.

track trading on the NDS-OM / OTC market and on the exchanges for
settlement, reconciliation, and management reporting.
requirements.object: track trading on the NDS-OM / OTC market and on the exchanges for settlement, reconciliation, and management reporting

A UCB shall use only a SEBI registered broker who is authorized by the
permitted exchanges (NSE / BSE) to undertake transactions in government
securities for placing buy / sell orders, subject to the guidelines on transactions
done through brokers as provided in paragraphs 134 to 139 of these Directions.
requirements.object: registered broker who is authorized by the permitted exchanges (NSE / BSE) to undertake transactions in government securities for placing buy / sell orders

A valid contract note indicating the time of execution shall be obtained from the
broker at end of day.
requirements.object: broker at end of day

requirements.full_sentence:
All transactions shall be monitored with a view to ensuring timely receipt of
funds and securities.
requirements.object: view to ensuring timely receipt of funds and securities

requirements.full_sentence:
Any delay or failure shall be promptly taken up with the
exchange / authorities concerned.
requirements.object: with the exchange / authorities concerned

requirements.full_sentence:
At the time of trade, securities shall be available with the UCB either in its SGL
or in the demat account with depositories, except as allowed in paragraph 86 of
these Directions.
requirements.object: UCB either in its SGL or in the demat account with depositories

requirements.full_sentence:
Any settlement failure on account of non-delivery of securities / non- availability
of clear funds shall be treated as SGL bouncing and the current penalties in
respect of SGL bouncing shall be applicable.
requirements.object: bouncing and the current penalties in respect of SGL bouncing shall be applicable

requirements.full_sentence:
Repo transactions (including reverse repo transactions) entered by a UCB shall
be subject to guidelines specified in Repurchase Transactions (Repo) (Reserve
Bank) Directions, 2018.
requirements.object: specified in Repurchase Transactions (Repo) (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2018

requirements.full_sentence:
A scheduled UCB shall have the option to undertake business of retailing of
government securities, provided that:
<image>
(1) Such retailing shall be on outright basis and there shall be no restriction
on the period between sale and purchase;
requirements.object: undertake business of retailing of government securities

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The retailing of government securities shall be on the basis of ongoing
market rates / yield curve emerging out of secondary market transactions;
requirements.object: of ongoing market rates / yield curve emerging out of secondary market transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(3) A UCB shall adhere to guidelines issued by RBI on retailing of
government securities, from time to time.
requirements.object: by RBI on retailing of government securities, from time to time

requirements.full_sentence:
Stripping / reconstitution of Government Securities shall be subject to the
Guidelines on Stripping / Reconstitution of Government Securities issued vide

Reconstitution in State Government Securities issued vide IDMD.RD.S390/

(1) STRIPS shall be valued and accounted for as ZCBs and in the manner
prescribed in these Directions;

(2) The discount rates used for valuation of STRIPS at inception shall be
market-based.

SGL account of
participant placing request for stripping shall be debited by the face value
of the government security and shall be simultaneously credited with the
aggregate face value of Coupon STRIPS (equal to the aggregate coupon
amounts) as well as the face value of Principal STRIPS (equal to the face
value of the government security);

account of the participant at their discounted value and at the same time,
the government security in question shall be derecognised.

<image>
accounting treatment for reconstitution shall be exactly the opposite of
stripping.

requirements.full_sentence:
The detailed procedure for accounting of STRIPS is given
below:
i The stripping / reconstitution shall not result in any profit or loss.

requirements.full_sentence:
The
present value of the STRIPS (coupon as well as principal) discounted
using the Zero Coupon Yield Curve (ZCYC) shall be normalized using
a factor that will be the ratio of the book value or market value of the
security (whichever is lower) to the sum total of the market value of all
STRIPS created out of the security.
requirements.object: factor that will be the ratio of the book value or market value of the security (whichever is lower) to the sum total of the mar

requirements.full_sentence:
iii In case STRIPS are created from securities held in the HTM portfolio,
the securities shall be transferred from the HTM category to the AFS /
HFT category as per these Directions.
requirements.object: HTM category to the AFS / HFT category as per these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
Thereafter, the lower of the
book value / market value shall be used for normalizing the market
value of individual STRIPS to the book value / market value.
requirements.object: the market value of individual STRIPS to the book value / market value

requirements.full_sentence:
Post
stripping, the book value / market value of the existing securities shall
be derecognized and replaced by the normalized value of STRIPS
whose sum total shall exactly equal the book value / market value of
the extinguished security (thereby ensuring that there is no profit or
loss on account of stripping).
requirements.object: by the normalized value of STRIPS whose sum total shall exactly equal the book value / market value of the extinguished s

Any appreciation, arising due to the
shifting of the security from HTM shall be ignored.

The same
methodology shall be followed for securities that are stripped from the

if any, shall be
recognised

depreciation shall not be aggregated for the purpose of arriving at net
depreciation / appreciation of investment under the AFS / HFT

requirements.full_sentence:

In the event the investment in the shares of co-operative institutions is valued at
₹1 per institution on account of the non-availability of the latest financial
position in accordance with paragraph 68(3) of these Directions, those shares
shall be reckoned as NPI.

conditions: If any credit facility availed by the issuer is NPA in the books of a UCB
requirements.full_sentence:
If any credit facility availed by the issuer is NPA in the books of a UCB,
investment in any of the securities issued by the same issuer shall also be
treated as NPI and vice versa.
requirements.object: NPI and vice versa

conditions: In case of conversion of principal and / or interest into equity
requirements.full_sentence:
In case of conversion of principal and / or interest into equity, debentures,
bonds, etc., such instruments shall be treated as NPA ab initio in the same
asset classification category as the loan, if the loan's classification is
substandard or doubtful on implementation of the restructuring package and
provision shall be made as per the norms.
requirements.object: ab initio in the same asset classification category as the loan

(1) Investment in State Government guaranteed securities, shall attract
prudential norms for identification of NPI and provisioning, when interest /
instalment of principal (including maturity proceeds) or any other amount
due to the UCB remains unpaid for more than 90 days.
requirements.object: identification of NPI and provisioning
